methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $19M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Aeon Industrial is a Seed-stage Defense & Security company founded by Naweed Tahmas that has raised $18.6M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 10 private companies and 2 public comparables in the defense-tech sector, including Series A-stage peers Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M), alongside Seed-stage peer Tenna Systems ($60M).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-Progression analysis, using the $18.6M raised as a proxy for current valuation, applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage progression, implying a $46M valuation. Peer-Median analysis across the four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $225M, with a range spanning $60M to $500M. The dispersion reflects stage heterogeneity within the peer set—three Series A companies trading at substantially higher valuations than the single Seed-stage reference point.

These methods triangulate to a valuation range of $40M to $259M, with a median of $136M. The wide dispersion and tension between the stage-progression method ($46M) and peer median ($225M) reflects material uncertainty around the appropriate comparable stage and Aeon's relative positioning within the Defense & Security market. Key dependencies include product-market fit validation, competitive differentiation relative to the peer set, and timing to next institutional funding round.
